title: C++ Multi-Dimensional Arrays
content:
### Multi-Dimensional Arrays

Multi-dimensional arrays store data in a grid-like structure with rows and columns.

---

### 2D Array Declaration

```cpp
int matrix[3][4];  // 3 rows, 4 columns

int grid[2][3] = {
  {1, 2, 3},
  {4, 5, 6}
};
```

---

### Accessing Elements

```cpp
int arr[2][3] = {{1, 2, 3}, {4, 5, 6}};

cout << arr[0][0];  // First row, first column: 1
cout << arr[1][2];  // Second row, third column: 6

arr[0][1] = 99;     // Modify element
```

---

### Iterating 2D Arrays

```cpp
int rows = 2, cols = 3;
for (int i = 0; i < rows; i++) {
  for (int j = 0; j < cols; j++) {
    cout << grid[i][j] << " ";
  }
  cout << endl;
}
```

---

### 3D Arrays

```cpp
int cube[2][3][4];  // 2 layers, 3 rows, 4 columns
```

---

### Memory Layout

2D arrays are stored in row-major order (all elements of row 0 come first):

```
[0][0] [0][1] [0][2] [1][0] [1][1] [1][2]
```

---

> **Key Point:** Multi-dimensional arrays in C++ are arrays of arrays. They are stored contiguously in row-major order in memory.

quiz.question: How is a 2D array stored in memory?
